mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth accelerates corporate earnings expectations, lifting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not exhibit a positive correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0%, labor market tightness reduces wage-driven inflation fears and supports consumer spending, boosting equity returns over a 1-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in >50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.0% within a 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production signals broad-based economic expansion, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of HOUST and INDPRO from their 12-month moving averages fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a decline in the federal funds rate signals a policy pivot that reduces discount rates and triggers positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS declines during tightening cycles do not produce statistically higher 20-day forward returns than baseline (t-stat < 1.96 over 3-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, real yields rise and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 1-week horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of periods where CPIAUCSL annualized growth surpasses 2.5% over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising non-farm payrolls combined with stable unemployment indicate healthy labor demand without overheating, supporting corporate profit margins and driving positive equity returns over a 1-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combination of PAYEMS growth > 200k and UNRATE < 6.5% fails to yield positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances across a 3-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and aggregate supply, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO quarter-over-quarter growth shows no positive correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month, consumer demand weakness signals earnings downgrades, producing negative equity returns over a 1-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when RSAFS MoM growth drops below 0.2% (t-stat > -1.65 over a 3-year rolling window).
